initial_symptoms: Patient presents with right lower quadrant (RLQ) pain, nausea, and fever.

exam_findings.general: Patient appears uncomfortable, guarding right side.

initial_presentation: 28M, acute abdominal pain x 12h, migrated from periumbilical to RLQ. No prior surgeries.

interview_responses.social_history: Drinks alcohol occasionally.
interview_responses.onset: Pain started around the belly button 12 hours ago, then moved to the right lower area.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with sudden onset of left-sided weakness and slurred speech.

exam_findings.neurological: Left facial droop, left arm drift, expressive aphasia. NIHSS score approximately 12.
exam_findings.cardiovascular: Irregular rhythm suggestive of atrial fibrillation.

test_results.ct_head: No acute hemorrhage. Early signs of ischemic changes in right MCA territory.

initial_presentation: 72M, witnessed onset of left-sided weakness and speech difficulty 45 minutes ago. Last known well at 0800.

interview_responses.social_history: Smokes 1 pack a day for 40 years.
interview_responses.onset: Wife says I was fine at breakfast, then suddenly couldn't move my left arm.
interview_responses.medications: Metformin 500mg twice daily, Lisinopril 10mg daily. Not taking any blood thinners.

initial_symptoms: Patient complaining of severe crushing chest pain radiating to the left arm, and diaphoresis.

exam_findings.chest: Clear to auscultation. Heart regular rate, clammy skin.
exam_findings.cardiovascular: S4 gallop noted. Jugular venous distension.

test_results.ecg: ST elevation in leads II, III, aVF indicating inferior STEMI.
test_results.chest_xray: No acute pulmonary pathology. Mild cardiomegaly.

initial_presentation: 58M, crushing substernal chest pain x 2h, radiating to left arm. Diaphoretic and anxious.

interview_responses.family_history: Extensive history of CAD. Father had MI at 55.
interview_responses.social_history: Inactive lifestyle. Former smoker.
interview_responses.onset: Started 2 hours ago while watching TV. Feels like an elephant on my chest.

initial_symptoms: Patient reports high fever, productive cough with rust-colored sputum, and chills for 3 days.

exam_findings.chest: Crackles and decreased breath sounds in the right lower lobe. Egophony present.
exam_findings.general: Appears acutely ill, flushed, using accessory muscles.

test_results.chest_xray: Dense consolidation in the right lower lobe with air bronchograms.
test_results.blood_culture: Pending. Gram stain shows gram-positive diplococci.

initial_presentation: 65F, productive cough with rust sputum x 3d, high fever, rigors. Lives alone.

interview_responses.social_history: Non-smoker. Lives independently.
interview_responses.onset: Started with a dry cough 5 days ago that became productive with colored sputum 3 days ago.

initial_symptoms: Patient complains of frequent watery diarrhea, abdominal cramping, and vomiting for 24 hours.

exam_findings.abdomen: Hyperactive bowel sounds, diffuse mild tenderness, poor skin turgor.
exam_findings.general: Dry mucous membranes, sunken eyes, delayed capillary refill.

test_results.stool_culture: Pending. Rapid viral panel positive for Norovirus.

initial_presentation: 34F, profuse watery diarrhea and vomiting x 24h. Unable to keep any fluids down.

interview_responses.social_history: Attended a potluck dinner 2 days ago. Others also fell ill.
interview_responses.onset: Sudden onset yesterday morning with nausea, then vomiting and diarrhea.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with acute onset of shortness of breath and pleuritic chest pain after a long flight.

exam_findings.chest: Clear lungs bilaterally. Tachycardia. Unilateral right calf swelling and warmth.
exam_findings.cardiovascular: Loud P2 component. Right ventricular heave.

test_results.ctpa: Large saddle embolus in the right main pulmonary artery with RV strain.
test_results.ecg: Sinus tachycardia, S1Q3T3 pattern, right axis deviation.

initial_presentation: 45F, sudden dyspnea and pleuritic chest pain 6h after 14h international flight. Right leg swelling.

interview_responses.social_history: On oral contraceptives for 5 years. Non-smoker.
interview_responses.onset: Was fine during the flight. Suddenly couldn't breathe about 6 hours after landing.
interview_responses.pain_scale: 6 out of 10, sharp, worse with deep breathing.

initial_symptoms: Patient describes frequent urination, excessive thirst, and unintentional weight loss over 2 weeks.

exam_findings.general: Fruity breath odor. Deep, rapid breathing (Kussmaul). Appears dehydrated and lethargic.

test_results.abg: pH 7.15, pCO2 20, HCO3 8 — primary metabolic acidosis with respiratory compensation

initial_presentation: 19M, polyuria/polydipsia x 2w, abdominal pain, Kussmaul breathing. No prior DM diagnosis.

interview_responses.social_history: College student. No alcohol or drug use.
interview_responses.onset: Been drinking a lot of water and going to the bathroom frequently for 2 weeks. Lost 5kg.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with severe, sharp, unilateral flank pain radiating to the groin, and hematuria.

exam_findings.abdomen: Right costovertebral angle (CVA) tenderness. Patient constantly shifting positions.

test_results.urine: Microscopic hematuria, no nitrites, no WBCs.
test_results.ct_abdomen: 5mm calculus in the right proximal ureter with mild hydronephrosis.

initial_presentation: 42M, acute colicky right flank pain radiating to groin. Pink-tinged urine. Cannot sit still.

interview_responses.social_history: Drinks minimal water. High-protein diet.
interview_responses.onset: Woke up at 3AM with excruciating pain in my right side.

initial_symptoms: Patient reports progressive headache, neck stiffness, and photophobia developing over 24 hours.

exam_findings.neurological: Positive Brudzinski's and Kernig's signs. Altered mental status. GCS 13 (E3V4M6).
exam_findings.general: Petechial rash on trunk and extremities.

test_results.lumbar_puncture: Cloudy CSF. WBC 2500 (95% neutrophils), Glucose 25 mg/dL (low), Protein 280 mg/dL (high).
test_results.ct_head: Normal, no increased ICP, no mass effect.

initial_presentation: 20M, severe headache x 24h, neck stiffness, photophobia, fever 102.5F. College dormitory resident.

interview_responses.social_history: College freshman living in dormitory. Roommate was recently ill.
interview_responses.onset: Started with a headache yesterday that kept getting worse. Light hurts my eyes.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with a red, swollen, intensely painful right big toe that started overnight.

exam_findings.extremity: Right first metatarsophalangeal joint is erythematous, warm, and exquisitely tender to touch.

test_results.joint_aspiration: Negatively birefringent needle-shaped crystals under polarized microscopy. WBC 20,000.

initial_presentation: 55M, acute onset painful swollen right great toe overnight. Reports heavy red meat consumption.

interview_responses.social_history: Drinks beer regularly. Had steak dinner last night.
interview_responses.onset: Woke up with my toe on fire. Can't even put a sheet on it.

initial_symptoms: Patient reports sudden onset tearing chest pain radiating to the back.

exam_findings.chest: Clear lungs. Diastolic murmur of aortic regurgitation. Asymmetric pulses.
exam_findings.cardiovascular: >20 mmHg blood pressure differential between arms.

test_results.ct_chest: Stanford Type A intimal flap seen in ascending aorta extending to arch.

initial_presentation: 62M, sudden tearing chest/back pain. BP asymmetry between arms. Known uncontrolled HTN.

interview_responses.family_history: Father had hypertension. Brother died suddenly at 50.
interview_responses.social_history: Smokes extensively. Heavy cocaine use in past.
interview_responses.onset: Felt like my chest was being ripped apart. Pain shot straight through to my back.

initial_symptoms: Female patient presents with severe right lower quadrant (RLQ) abdominal pain and lightheadedness.

exam_findings.abdomen: Severe RLQ tenderness with guarding. Pale skin.
exam_findings.pelvic: Cervical motion tenderness, right adnexal mass palpable.

test_results.ultrasound_pelvis: Empty uterus, complex mass in right adnexa with free fluid in pelvis.
test_results.blood_cbc: Hemoglobin 7.0 g/dL (Low, acute hemorrhage). Type and crossmatch ordered.

initial_presentation: 29F, acute RLQ pain, syncope, hypotension. LMP 7 weeks ago. Tachycardic.

interview_responses.social_history: Last menstrual period was 7 weeks ago. Previous ectopic pregnancy 2 years ago.
interview_responses.onset: Sharp pain started 3 hours ago and has been getting worse. I fainted once.
interview_responses.medications: No current medications. Not on contraception.

initial_symptoms: Infant brought in by parents with high-pitched crying, drawing up legs, and episodes of lethargy with bloody stool.

exam_findings.abdomen: Palpable sausage-shaped mass in right upper quadrant. Distended. Dance's sign positive (empty RLQ).
exam_findings.general: Infant alternates between inconsolable crying and lethargy. Pale.

test_results.ultrasound_abdomen: Target sign (concentric rings) consistent with ileocolic intussusception. No perforation.

initial_presentation: 9-month-old M, episodic crying with leg drawing x 8h, currant jelly stool, intermittent lethargy.

interview_responses.family_history: No family history of GI disorders.
interview_responses.social_history: Fully vaccinated. No recent illness.
interview_responses.onset: Started crying and pulling up legs this morning. Had one bloody stool that looked like jelly.

initial_symptoms: Elderly patient found on floor, confused, with right hip pain and inability to bear weight.

exam_findings.extremity: Right leg shortened and externally rotated. Severe pain with passive range of motion.
exam_findings.neurological: Confused, oriented to person only. CAM positive for delirium.
exam_findings.general: Dry mucous membranes. Multiple bruises on arms.

test_results.xray_hip: Displaced subcapital femoral neck fracture, right.
test_results.urine: UTI screen: positive nitrites, positive leukocyte esterase.

initial_presentation: 82F, found on floor, confused, right hip pain, unable to walk. Lives alone. Baseline dementia.

interview_responses.social_history: Lives alone. Home health aide visits 3x/week. Baseline mild cognitive impairment.
interview_responses.onset: Neighbor found me this morning. I don't remember falling.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with sudden severe headache described as 'the worst headache of my life', with brief loss of consciousness.

exam_findings.neurological: Nuchal rigidity. GCS 13 (E3V4M6). Left pupil sluggish. No focal motor deficits.

test_results.ct_head: Diffuse subarachnoid hemorrhage in basal cisterns. Fisher Grade 3.
test_results.ct_angiogram: 6mm saccular aneurysm at the anterior communicating artery.

initial_presentation: 48F, thunderclap headache with syncope. Worst headache of life. Vomiting. Meningismus.

interview_responses.family_history: Mother died of brain aneurysm rupture.
interview_responses.social_history: Non-smoker. Moderate alcohol use.
interview_responses.onset: Was exercising when I felt a sudden explosion in my head. I passed out briefly.
interview_responses.pain_scale: 10 out of 10. Worst headache I have ever had.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with progressive dyspnea, wheezing, barrel chest, and chronic productive cough worsening over 3 days.

exam_findings.chest: Diffuse expiratory wheezes and rhonchi bilaterally. Prolonged expiratory phase. Accessory muscle use. Pursed lip breathing.
exam_findings.general: Cyanotic nail beds. Appears in moderate respiratory distress.

test_results.abg: pH 7.32, pCO2 55, pO2 52, HCO3 28 — acute on chronic respiratory acidosis.
test_results.chest_xray: Hyperinflated lungs with flattened diaphragms. Right lower lobe infiltrate suggesting superimposed infection.
test_results.sputum_culture: Gram stain: gram-negative rods. Culture pending.

initial_presentation: 70M, COPD on home O2, worsening dyspnea x 3d, increased sputum (now purulent). SpO2 85% on RA.

interview_responses.social_history: 40 pack-year smoking history. Quit 5 years ago. On home oxygen 2L/min.
interview_responses.onset: My breathing has been getting worse for 3 days. Coughing up thick green stuff.
interview_responses.medications: Tiotropium, Advair, Home oxygen 2L nasal cannula.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with acute confusion, muscle rigidity, and high fever shortly after starting a new antipsychotic medication.

exam_findings.neurological: Severe lead-pipe rigidity in all extremities. Altered mental status. Diaphoresis.

initial_presentation: 35M, new onset confusion, rigidity, hyperthermia 106F. Started haloperidol 3 days ago. Diaphoretic.

interview_responses.social_history: Lives in group home. Recently started new antipsychotic (haloperidol) 3 days ago.
interview_responses.onset: Staff noticed he became confused and stiff yesterday. Temperature kept climbing.
interview_responses.pain_scale: Unable to assess due to altered mental status.
interview_responses.medications: Haloperidol 5mg IM, started 3 days ago. Previously on risperidone.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with severe epigastric pain radiating to the back, nausea, and vomiting after heavy alcohol binge.

exam_findings.abdomen: Severe epigastric tenderness with guarding. Decreased bowel sounds. Cullen's sign (periumbilical bruising).

test_results.lipase: Lipase 1,250 U/L (>3x upper normal limit, diagnostic for acute pancreatitis).
test_results.bmp: BUN 28, Glucose 220, Calcium 7.8 (low — Ranson criteria positive).
test_results.ct_abdomen: Edematous pancreas with peripancreatic fluid collection. No necrosis.

initial_presentation: 44M, severe epigastric pain to back after heavy drinking. Vomiting x 6h. Jaundiced.

interview_responses.social_history: Heavy drinker, 12+ beers per day. Previous episodes of similar pain.
interview_responses.onset: Pain started after drinking heavily last night. Went straight through to my back.
interview_responses.pain_scale: 10 out of 10. Leaning forward helps slightly.
interview_responses.medications: None. Does not take prescribed medications.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with severe allergic reaction: diffuse urticaria, lip/tongue swelling, and difficulty breathing after eating shellfish.

exam_findings.airway: Marked angioedema of lips, tongue, and uvula. Stridor. Difficulty swallowing.
exam_findings.skin: Generalized urticaria and flushing over entire body.

test_results.tryptase: Serum tryptase elevated (confirms mast cell degranulation).
test_results.abg: pH 7.38, pCO2 32, pO2 62 — hypoxemia with mild respiratory alkalosis.

initial_presentation: 32F, diffuse hives, lip swelling, wheezing, hypotension after eating shrimp 20 min ago. Stridor.

interview_responses.allergies: Known shellfish allergy — accidentally consumed shrimp in a dish.
interview_responses.social_history: Carries EpiPen but left it at home today.
interview_responses.onset: About 20 minutes after eating. Started with itching, then lips swelled up, now hard to breathe.
interview_responses.pain_scale: 3 out of 10, throat tightness is the main concern.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with progressive weakness, fatigue, hyperpigmentation of skin creases, and dizziness upon standing.

exam_findings.general: Hyperpigmented skin especially in palmar creases and buccal mucosa. Cachectic. Orthostatic hypotension (>20 mmHg drop on standing).

test_results.acth: ACTH > 200 pg/mL (markedly elevated — primary adrenal insufficiency).

initial_presentation: 38F, progressive fatigue x 3mo, now acutely hypotensive, confused. Dark skin pigmentation. Salt craving.

interview_responses.family_history: Sister has lupus. Mother has hypothyroidism.
interview_responses.social_history: Has been craving salt for months. Noticed skin getting darker.
interview_responses.onset: Been feeling weak for months but got much worse this week. Almost fainted twice today.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with upper GI bleeding: hematemesis (coffee-ground emesis) and melena.

exam_findings.abdomen: Epigastric tenderness, hepatomegaly. Spider angiomata on chest. Palmar erythema.
exam_findings.general: Pale, diaphoretic. Signs of chronic liver disease.

initial_presentation: 56M, vomiting blood (coffee-ground), black tarry stools x 2. Known cirrhosis. Hypotensive.

interview_responses.social_history: Active alcoholic. Diagnosed with cirrhosis 2 years ago. Previous variceal banding.
interview_responses.onset: Started vomiting dark blood this morning. Had 3 episodes. Black stool for 2 days.

initial_symptoms: Child presents with barking cough, inspiratory stridor, and low-grade fever for 2 days.

exam_findings.airway: Audible inspiratory stridor at rest. Barking seal-like cough. Mild intercostal retractions.
exam_findings.chest: Transmitted upper airway sounds. No wheezing. Air entry adequate bilaterally.

test_results.neck_xray: Steeple sign — subglottic narrowing consistent with croup.

initial_presentation: 3-year-old M, barking cough x 2d, stridor at rest, low fever. URI symptoms preceded.

interview_responses.social_history: Attends daycare. Several children in class have been sick.
interview_responses.onset: Had a runny nose 3 days ago, then developed this barking cough. Worse at night.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with sudden painless loss of vision in the right eye described as a curtain being pulled down.

exam_findings.ophthalmologic: Right eye: absent direct pupillary reflex (Marcus Gunn pupil). Fundoscopy: pale retina with cherry-red spot at macula. Box-car segmentation of retinal vessels.

test_results.carotid_doppler: 70% stenosis of right internal carotid artery.
test_results.ecg: Normal sinus rhythm. No atrial fibrillation.
test_results.esr: 12 mm/hr (normal, rules out giant cell arteritis).

initial_presentation: 68M, sudden painless complete vision loss right eye x 2h. Like a curtain fell. HTN, carotid bruit.

interview_responses.social_history: Former smoker. Takes medications regularly.
interview_responses.onset: Was reading the newspaper and suddenly the vision in my right eye went completely black.
interview_responses.pain_scale: 0 out of 10. No pain at all.

initial_symptoms: Diabetic patient presents with crushing chest pain, nausea, and shortness of breath. ECG shows new LBBB.

test_results.ecg: New left bundle branch block (LBBB). Previously normal ECG on file.

initial_presentation: 65M, diabetic, crushing chest pain, SOB, new LBBB on ECG. Bilateral crackles. S3 gallop.

interview_responses.family_history: Father died of MI at 58. Mother has diabetes.
interview_responses.social_history: Former smoker. Sedentary. Non-compliant with diet.
interview_responses.onset: Chest felt heavy 4 hours ago. Got worse, started sweating and couldn't breathe lying down.
interview_responses.medications: Metformin 1000mg BID, Glipizide 5mg, Lisinopril 20mg, Aspirin 81mg, Atorvastatin 40mg.

initial_symptoms: Patient found unresponsive with pinpoint pupils, shallow breathing, and a used syringe nearby.

exam_findings.neurological: GCS 3 (E1V1M1). Pinpoint pupils bilaterally. No response to pain.
exam_findings.airway: Shallow, agonal breathing. Cyanotic lips and extremities.
exam_findings.general: Track marks on bilateral antecubital fossae.

test_results.urine_tox: Positive for opioids, negative for benzodiazepines.
test_results.abg: pH 7.18, pCO2 72, pO2 45 — severe respiratory acidosis with hypoxemia.

initial_presentation: 27M, found unresponsive, pinpoint pupils, RR 6, SpO2 78%. Syringe at scene. Track marks.

interview_responses.social_history: Known IV heroin user per EMS. Multiple prior overdose reversals.
interview_responses.onset: Found by roommate approximately 30 minutes ago. Unknown substance or amount.
interview_responses.medications: Unknown. Was previously on buprenorphine but stopped.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with sudden onset of severe testicular pain, nausea, and a high-riding left testicle.

exam_findings.genitourinary: Left testicle is high-riding, horizontally oriented (bell-clapper deformity). Absent cremasteric reflex. Exquisitely tender. Negative Prehn's sign.

test_results.doppler_ultrasound_testicular: Absent blood flow to left testicle. Right testicle normal.
test_results.urine: No WBCs, no bacteria (rules out epididymitis).

initial_presentation: 16M, acute left testicular pain x 3h, nausea. High-riding testicle. Absent cremasteric reflex.

interview_responses.social_history: High school student. Was playing basketball when pain started.
interview_responses.onset: Sudden sharp pain in my left testicle 3 hours ago during a basketball game.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with gradually worsening bilateral leg swelling, facial puffiness, and foamy urine.

exam_findings.general: Periorbital edema, anasarca. 3+ pitting edema bilateral lower extremities.
exam_findings.chest: Decreased breath sounds at bilateral bases (pleural effusions).

test_results.urine: Protein 4+ (nephrotic range). Urine protein/creatinine ratio > 3.5 g/day equivalent. Lipiduria with oval fat bodies.
test_results.renal_biopsy: Minimal change disease on electron microscopy (foot process effacement).

initial_presentation: 25M, progressive generalized edema x 3w, foamy urine, weight gain 8kg. BP 150/95.

interview_responses.social_history: Recently recovered from upper respiratory infection 4 weeks ago.
interview_responses.onset: Noticed my eyes were puffy in the mornings 3 weeks ago. Legs started swelling. Urine looks foamy.
interview_responses.pain_scale: 1 out of 10. Just uncomfortable from swelling.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents with high fever, diffuse erythroderma (sunburn-like rash), hypotension, and confusion during menstruation.

exam_findings.skin: Diffuse macular erythroderma (sunburn-like rash). Desquamation beginning on palms and soles.
exam_findings.general: Acutely ill, confused, oliguric. Tampon present on exam.
exam_findings.mucous_membranes: Hyperemia of conjunctivae, oropharynx, and vaginal mucosa.

test_results.bmp: Creatinine 3.2, BUN 55 (renal failure). AST 250, ALT 180 (liver involvement).
test_results.blood_culture: Staphylococcus aureus isolated. TSST-1 toxin positive.

initial_presentation: 22F, high fever, diffuse red rash, hypotension, confusion. Menstruating with tampon in place x 3 days.

interview_responses.social_history: College student. Has been using super-absorbent tampons. Current tampon in for 3 days.
interview_responses.onset: Started feeling flu-like yesterday. Rash appeared overnight. Got confused this morning.
interview_responses.pain_scale: Unable to assess reliably due to altered mental status.

initial_symptoms: Patient presents after a motor vehicle collision with chest pain, shortness of breath, and distended neck veins.

exam_findings.chest: Absent breath sounds on the left. Tracheal deviation to the right. Hyperresonance to percussion on left.
exam_findings.cardiovascular: Distended jugular veins. Muffled heart sounds.
exam_findings.general: Cyanotic, severe respiratory distress, accessory muscle use.

test_results.chest_xray: Large left tension pneumothorax with mediastinal shift to the right. No hemothorax.
test_results.fast_exam: No free fluid in abdomen. No pericardial effusion.

initial_presentation: 30M, MVC high-speed. Chest pain, dyspnea, absent left breath sounds, JVD, tracheal deviation right.

interview_responses.family_history: Unable to obtain — patient in extremis.
interview_responses.social_history: Restrained driver, head-on collision at approximately 50 mph.
interview_responses.onset: Immediately after the crash. Getting harder to breathe every minute.

initial_symptoms: Elderly diabetic patient presents with fever, right flank pain, pyuria, and altered mental status.

exam_findings.general: Confused, flushed, warm extremities (hyperdynamic septic shock). Dry mucous membranes.
exam_findings.skin: Mottled extremities. Delayed capillary refill >3 seconds.

test_results.urine: Turbid, WBC >100/hpf, positive nitrites, positive leukocyte esterase. Many gram-negative rods.
test_results.blood_culture: Pending. Gram stain: gram-negative rods (likely E. coli).

initial_presentation: 78F, diabetic, fever 103.8F, confusion, right flank pain, hypotension. Lactate 4.8. Pyuria.

interview_responses.social_history: Nursing home resident. Foley catheter in place. Multiple prior UTIs.
interview_responses.onset: Staff noted confusion and fever starting yesterday. She complained of back pain.
interview_responses.pain_scale: Unable to reliably assess due to confusion.
interview_responses.medications: Insulin Glargine 20 units nightly, Metformin 500mg BID, Lisinopril 10mg.
